mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value of expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that leads equity investors to price in recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following triggered UNRATE increases exceeding 0.3pp, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances across the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of decelerating PAYEMS growth combined with a simultaneous uptick in UNRATE constitutes a stronger recession signal than either series alone, because payroll deceleration confirms the unemployment signal is not noise; this joint deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-horizon risk-off positioning accelerates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND UNRATE up month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint trigger condition shows no statistically significant negative mean 5-day forward return (t-test p > 0.10) versus unconditional baseline over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing recovery regime, sustained increases in HOUST above its trailing 12-month average signal expanding construction activity and household formation that feed through to broader consumption and corporate earnings over a full quarter, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with HOUST above its 12-month trailing average, mean 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly greater than unconditional mean (p > 0.10) over the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and improving corporate revenue prospects, generating a modest but immediate positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days as investors incorporate the incremental production data.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month change and forward 5d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth month-over-month exceeds 0.5%, it signals robust consumer spending momentum that supports corporate revenue beats in the following month, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following RSAFS triggers above 0.5% MoM growth, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound while CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above trend, the combination signals building pressure for future policy tightening that markets have not yet priced, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as valuation multiples compress in anticipation.
regime_hint: zero-rate policy regime with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: Under the joint trigger condition, mean 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) relative to unconditional baseline over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a labor market tightening regime, sustained month-over-month PAYEMS gains signal durable income growth and consumer confidence that supports equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods of positive PAYEMS momentum, 20-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive mean difference (p > 0.10) versus periods of flat or negative PAYEMS momentum.
